title: Boat Rental in Azores: A 2026 Guide to Prices and Tips
html:
Planning a boat rental in Azores offers a unique way to explore volcanic islands and deep-blue calderas. From Ponta Delgada Marina, a half-day motorboat hire typically ranges from EUR 250 to EUR 500. BoatHire24 provides a convenient platform to compare verified options across the archipelago.

Where to set off in Azores

Most visitors begin at Marina de Ponta Delgada on São Miguel Island, a full-service port with fuel, water and chandleries. Other strong departure points include Horta Marina on Faial, popular with transatlantic sailors, and Marina da Praia da Vitória on Terceira. For a quieter launch, consider Vila do Porto on Santa Maria or the natural anchorage at Angra do Heroísmo bay.

Each marina offers easy access to different island groups. From Horta, you can reach the channel to Pico island in about 5 nautical miles. Booking via the boat hire Azores page lets you filter by departure port and boat type.

Which boat to rent

Choosing the right vessel depends on your group size, experience and itinerary. Here is a curated list of popular hire options.

  1. Motorboat (6-8m), Best for coastal exploring and cave visits, carries up to 6 guests, from EUR 300 per half-day.
  2. RIB (Rigid Inflatable Boat), Fast and nimble for dolphin-watching trips, seats up to 8 guests, from EUR 400 for a full day.
  3. Sailing yacht (10-14m), Ideal for couples or families wanting a peaceful day sail, accommodates up to 8 guests, from EUR 600 for a full day without skipper.
  4. Catamaran (12-16m), Stable and spacious, perfect for larger groups or celebrations, up to 12 guests, from EUR 900 per full day.
  5. Crewed luxury motor yacht (16m+), All-inclusive skipper, host and meals, carries up to 10 guests, from EUR 1,500 for a full day. Models include the Maiora 26m and Azimut 50, see for example the Limpopo listing for an Axopar 28.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Azores?

Prices vary widely by season, boat size and level of service. Here is a breakdown of typical 2026 costs in EUR.

  • Shared small-group tour (RIB, 2, 3 hours): EUR 60, 100 per person.
  • Small motorboat (up to 6m) half-day self-drive: EUR 250, 400.
  • Catamaran (12, 16m) full-day with skipper: EUR 800, 1,200.
  • Premium crewed yacht (16m+, full day including crew and catering): EUR 1,500, 3,000.

These estimates assume high season. BoatHire24 lists prices per boat and allows you to compare extras such as fuel, insurance and skipper fees.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Azores?

For most motorboats up to 15m, a valid ICC (International Certificate of Competence) or equivalent is required. Portuguese legislation recognises foreign licences as long as they include a coastal endorsement. For smaller RIBs under 6m and limited horsepower, some operators offer bareboat hire without a licence, but always ask the operator directly. The local rule is that the skipper must carry an appropriate qualification; the operator confirms exactly which papers you need.

Avoid anchoring in seagrass beds, which are protected Marine Protected Areas. Use the mooring buoys provided in places like Ilhéu de Vila Franca do Campo; they protect the delicate ecosystem and your boat's hull.

When is the best season to rent a boat in Azores?

The peak season runs from June to September, when weather is warmest and winds are lightest. July and August offer consistent northerly winds, ideal for sailing, while May and October can also be excellent with fewer crowds. The Azores high-pressure system brings long sunny days, but always check real-time forecasts at the marina office.

Booking in advance for summer 2026 is recommended, especially for crewed yachts. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Azores or list your own.

At a glance

  • Price band: EUR 250, 3,000 per half/full day
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Typical trip length: half-day (4h) or full-day (8h)
  • Maximum capacity: up to 12 guests on most boats
  • Currency: EUR
  • Licence: ICC or equivalent for self-drive; optional crew available

Questions about boat rental in Azores

Can I rent a boat in Azores without a licence?

Yes, you can charter a skippered boat or a small unlicensed motorboat under 6m with low horsepower, but confirm with the operator as rules vary.

What is the best island for a day boat trip from Ponta Delgada?

Set off from Ponta Delgada Marina and head to Ilhéu de Vila Franca do Campo, a protected islet about 18 nautical miles away, ideal for swimming and snorkelling.

Are there hidden fees when booking a boat in Azores?

Always check the listing for included items such as fuel, mooring fees and lifejackets; some operators charge extra for skipper or port departure tax.

How far can I travel in a single day on a chartered boat?

With a fast motorboat (20-30 knots) you can comfortably reach islands 15-20 nautical miles away, such as from São Miguel to Santa Maria, but allow at least 30 minutes each way.

Whether you are exploring the volcanic craters of Sete Cidades from the water or island-hopping between Faial and Pico, planning your trip starts with a reliable booking. Visit BoatHire24 to browse verified boats and read owner profiles. If you own a boat in Azores, consider listing it on BoatHire24 to reach travellers worldwide.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Azores and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
